title: asperula lilaciflora subsp. coa
intro: Asperula lilaciflora subsp. coa, also known as Coa Woodruff, is a subspecies of the Asperula lilaciflora plant, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. It is a perennial herb native to the Iberian Peninsula and North Africa, known for its medicinal properties and traditional use in herbal medicine. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate digestive issues, improve cardiovascular health, and reduce stress levels. Coa Woodruff can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies and natural health practices.
